title: AST Deceased Person: North Pole [AK25092603]
summary: On 09/11/25 AST received a report that Emil Bergman of North Pole was found deceased in his home. After investigation, it was determined he passed due to natural causes and no foul play was suspected. Next of kin have been notified. SME released the body to Fairbanks Funeral Home.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: A Successful Summer 2025 with SYP
summary: Each spring Kawerak begins to promote the Summer Youth Work Program (SYP), looking for youth eager to gain paid work experience. The program is open to tribally enrolled youth ages 14-24 in the Bering Strait Region, and jobs vary depending on the location and what worksites are available in the community. This year in 2025 the pro...

title: Trump Education Department to divert grants from colleges serving students of color
summary: File photo of the University of Nevada, Las Vegas, which is among the nation's largest Hispanic-serving institutions. Hispanic-serving institutions and other colleges and universities serving students of color will lose funding under a recent U.S. Education Department decision. (Photo by Hugh Jackson/Nevada Current) WASHINGTON — The U.S. Department of Education announced it will withhold $350 million of congressionally approved funds to minority-serving colleges and universities and divert the funds elsewhere, saying that the i...

title: Energy, Wildfire, Budget Update, and lots more!
summary: Dear friends and neighbors, Senate District E is big: from Tudor Road to Whittier! It encompasses 10 community council areas plus Girdwood Board of Supervisors. The post Energy, Wildfire, Budget Update, and lots more! appeared first on Alaska Senate Majority .

title: AST Found Property: Delta Junction [AK25092565]
summary: On 9-11, Delta AST received a report from Pogo Mine a handgun had been located near mile 40 of the Pogo Mine Rd. The handgun is at the Delta AST post. If you lost your handgun and can provide proof of ownership, the handgun can be picked up at the Delta AST post.

title: Alaska judge rules in state’s favor on repeal of a rule intended to limit health care costs
summary: (Getty Images) An Anchorage Superior Court judge’s ruling has cleared the way for the state of Alaska to repeal its “80th Percentile Rule,” enacted by the state in 2004 as part of an attempt to reduce health care costs in the state. title: AST Take Moose Closed Season/ Unlawful Possession and Transport: Glennallen [AK25090397]
incident_type: Take Moose Closed Season/ Unlawful Possession and Transport
summary: On 09/05/2025 at approximately 1705 hours, Shaunivon Vorhes, age 39 of Eagle River, was contacted by Alaska Wildlife Troopers after hunting for moose in Eureka. During the contact, it was discovered that Vorhes had harvested an illegal bull moose and was transporting it out of the field. Vorhes was charged with two misdemeanors for Take Moose Closed Season due to there being no open season for the size of the moose harvested and Unlawful Possession and Transport of Big Game Meat. He was issued a mandatory court date in October in the Glennallen Court to appear on the charges.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: Take Moose Closed Season/ Unlawful Possession and Transport

charges: two misdemeanors for Take Moose Closed Season due to there being no open season for the size of the moose harvested
entities.address_detail: Vorhes had harvested an illegal bull moose and was transpo

title: Resilience in Bloom: Flowers at the Heart of Arctic Security
summary: Arctic flowers play a key, yet often unrecognised role in Arctic security. However, climate shifts endanger Arctic flowers. The post Resilience in Bloom: Flowers at the Heart of Arctic Security appeared first on The Arctic Institute - Center for Circumpolar Security Studies .
